front: What are Iceberg production best practices — table maintenance, catalog choice, and operational patterns?
back: Catalog choice: use REST catalog for multi-engine flexibility; use Glue catalog if AWS-only and want zero-ops; avoid Hadoop catalog (filesystem-based) in production — no concurrent write safety, no service discovery. Table maintenance (schedule all three): (1) Compaction daily or hourly for streaming tables — merge small files into 256MB-512MB targets. (2) Expire snapshots weekly — keep 7-30 days based on time travel requirements. (3) Remove orphan files weekly with a 3-day buffer. Partitioning: always use hidden partitioning transforms (days/hours); partition by the most common filter column. File format: Parquet for analytics (columnar, compressed); ORC occasionally for Hive compatibility. Write mode: start with CoW for simplicity, migrate to MoR for streaming-heavy tables. Monitoring: track snapshot count (should stabilize after expiration), data file count per partition (compaction effectiveness), and metadata file size. Avoid writing too frequently — each commit creates metadata overhead. Batch commits every 60-300 seconds rather than per-event. Document your table's SLA: how fresh is the data, how long is time travel retained, what's the compaction schedule.
